title: Design Methods for Determining the bearing capacity of shallow footings based on pressuremeter test data
authors_names: Mohammed Baqer A. Azeez ، Jaffer M. Mohammed Baqer
description: Practical design methods for shallow foundations using Pressuremeter test results, parameter selection, and application notes, Includes calculation workflow and interpretation guidance
descriptionLong: This research presents practical design methods for determining the bearing capacity of shallow foundations directly from pressuremeter test (PMT) data. It covers the full interpretation workflow from raw field readings to design parameters, including limit pressure (Pl) and pressuremeter modulus (EM) selection, correlation with classical bearing-capacity theory, and the application of K-factor charts. The work compares several interpretation approaches, discusses sensitivity to soil state and layering, and provides a step-by-step calculation workflow with worked examples. Reporting templates and documentation guidance are included to support consistent application in real foundation projects.

title: Bearing capacity evaluation using pressuremeter limit pressure
description: A compact review of bearing capacity estimates derived from pressuremeter limit pressure and practical correction factors. Includes worked methodology outline and assumptions.
descriptionLong: A compact yet thorough review of how bearing capacity for shallow foundations can be estimated directly from the pressuremeter limit pressure. The study outlines the theoretical basis, presents practical correction factors for embedment and shape, and discusses the assumptions and limitations of the method. A worked methodology outline is provided so practitioners can reproduce the calculation chain from field data to allowable bearing pressure.

title: Shallow footing design workflow from pressuremeter modulus and PL
description: Step-by-step workflow connecting pressuremeter modulus and limit pressure to shallow footing sizing and serviceability checks. Notes on parameter selection and reporting.
descriptionLong: A step-by-step design workflow that links the pressuremeter modulus (EM) and limit pressure (PL) to shallow footing sizing and serviceability verification. The paper details parameter selection, settlement estimation, and the checks required to satisfy both strength and serviceability limit states. Practical notes on reporting and parameter justification accompany each stage of the workflow.

title: Comparative study of K-factor charts in pressuremeter foundation design
description: Comparison between common K-factor chart interpretations, sensitivity to soil state, and implications for design outcomes. Structured summary and recommended documentation practice.
descriptionLong: A comparative study examining the most widely used K-factor charts applied in pressuremeter-based foundation design. The work quantifies how chart selection and soil-state assumptions influence the resulting bearing capacity, highlights cases where interpretations diverge, and recommends a consistent documentation practice. The structured summary helps engineers choose defensible design values and understand the associated uncertainty.

title: Serviceability settlement checks for shallow foundations using pressuremeter data
description: Settlement and serviceability verification approach built on pressuremeter modulus interpretation with practical limits and reporting. Includes notes for layered profiles.
descriptionLong: This study focuses on serviceability settlement checks for shallow foundations using pressuremeter data. It develops a settlement-estimation approach based on pressuremeter modulus interpretation, defines practical settlement limits, and addresses the special considerations required for layered soil profiles. Clear reporting guidance is provided so that serviceability conclusions can be reproduced and audited.

title: Practical parameter selection in pressuremeter-based shallow foundation design
description: Guidance on selecting representative parameters (PL, EM, OCR adjustments) for design and consistency in technical reporting. Includes checklist-style recommendations.
descriptionLong: Practical guidance for selecting representative geotechnical parameters in pressuremeter-based shallow foundation design. The paper discusses how to derive and justify limit pressure (PL), pressuremeter modulus (EM), and overconsolidation adjustments, and how parameter choices propagate into design outcomes. A checklist-style set of recommendations promotes consistency across projects and reviewers.

title: Unified reporting template for pressuremeter test results in foundation projects
description: A clean template for presenting pressuremeter results, assumptions, derived parameters, and foundation design outputs. Designed for consistent project documentation.
descriptionLong: A unified reporting template designed to standardize how pressuremeter test results are presented in foundation projects. It organizes raw data, interpretation assumptions, derived design parameters, and final foundation outputs into a single consistent format. The template improves traceability, eases peer review, and reduces ambiguity between field, interpretation, and design stages.
